name: NASA SRTMGL1 Version 3 Global 1 arc-second DEM
provider: NASA LP DAAC / USGS
theme_raw: SRTM 1 arc-second global elevation
license: NASA Earthdata open data; redistribution allowed with citation
formats: HGT, GeoTIFF via Earthdata/OpenTopography/GEE
access: Earthdata download, OpenTopography API, Google Earth Engine asset USGS/SRTMGL1_003
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/MEaSUREs/SRTM/SRTMGL1.003
time_span: SRTM mission February 2000; Version 3 release
notes: Canonical NASA/USGS 30 m land DEM backbone; GEE and OpenTopography provide common analysis/download routes.
desc_zh: SRTMGL1 v003 是 NASA/USGS 发布的全球 1 角秒数字高程模型，主要覆盖约北纬 60 度至南纬 56 度陆地区域。它是地形、水文、灾害、生态和遥感几何校正中最常用的开放 DEM 基线之一，可通过 NASA Earthdata、OpenTopography 和 Google Earth Engine 等渠道访问。
desc_en: SRTMGL1 v003 is the NASA/USGS 1 arc-second global digital elevation model derived from the Shuttle Radar Topography Mission. It covers most land areas between about 60N and 56S and is a core open DEM baseline for terrain analysis, hydrology, hazards, ecology, and remote-sensing preprocessing. Access routes include NASA Earthdata, OpenTopography, and the Google Earth Engine asset USGS/SRTMGL1_003.
openness: open with free Earthdata account for some routes
source_type: official DOI and cloud analysis mirror
evidence: NASA DOI landing page; GEE asset USGS/SRTMGL1_003; OpenTopography global SRTM access

provider: NASA LP DAAC / USGS
theme_raw: SRTM 3 arc-second global elevation
license: NASA Earthdata open data; redistribution allowed with citation
formats: HGT, GeoTIFF via Earthdata/OpenTopography
access: Earthdata download and OpenTopography API
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/MEaSUREs/SRTM/SRTMGL3.003
time_span: SRTM mission February 2000; Version 3 release
notes: Coarser 90 m companion to SRTMGL1, useful for legacy workflows and lower-volume global processing.
desc_zh: SRTMGL3 v003 是 NASA/USGS 的 3 角秒全球 DEM，是 30 m SRTM 产品的较粗分辨率配套版本。它适合低数据量全球制图、旧模型复现和区域尺度地形分析，仍保留 SRTM 的稳定引用与官方下载链路。
desc_en: SRTMGL3 v003 is the 3 arc-second companion to the NASA/USGS SRTM global DEM family. The product is useful for lower-volume global processing, legacy model reproduction, and regional terrain applications where 90 m resolution is sufficient. It retains official DOI-backed access through NASA Earthdata and related distribution services.
openness: open with free Earthdata account for some routes
source_type: official DOI
evidence: NASA DOI landing page and Earthdata distribution

time_span: SRTM-era elevation with improved auxiliary processing
notes: Reprocessed SRTM-based DEM with improved void filling and auxiliary data integration; important modern replacement for many SRTM workflows.
desc_zh: NASADEM HGT v001 是 NASA 对 SRTM 高程进行再处理后的全球 DEM，改进了空洞填补、控制数据和辅助资料融合。它常被作为 SRTMGL1 的现代替代或补充，可通过 Earthdata 和 Google Earth Engine 访问。
desc_en: NASADEM HGT v001 is NASA's reprocessed SRTM-based global elevation product, improving void filling, control, and auxiliary-data integration relative to older SRTM releases. It is commonly used as a modern replacement or complement to SRTMGL1 in terrain, hydrology, and remote-sensing workflows. Access is available through Earthdata and Google Earth Engine.
openness: open with free Earthdata account for some routes
source_type: official DOI and GEE mirror
evidence: NASA DOI landing page; GEE asset NASA/NASADEM_HGT/001

notes: Optical stereo DEM complementing radar DEMs, with broader high-latitude land coverage than SRTM but different artifact characteristics.
desc_zh: ASTER GDEM v3 是由 METI/NASA ASTER 立体像对生成的全球 1 角秒 DEM，覆盖约 83N 至 83S 陆地。它与 SRTM/NASADEM 具有不同误差来源和覆盖优势，常用于多源高程比较、高纬地区补充和地形制图。
desc_en: ASTER GDEM Version 3 is a global 1 arc-second DEM generated from METI/NASA ASTER stereo imagery, covering land areas from roughly 83N to 83S. It complements radar-derived DEMs such as SRTM and NASADEM with different error characteristics and broader high-latitude coverage. The product is distributed through NASA Earthdata and mirrored in cloud analysis environments.
openness: open with Earthdata access and citation
source_type: official DOI and GEE mirror
evidence: NASA DOI landing page; LP DAAC ASTER GDEM record; GEE ASTER elevation assets

desc_en: Copernicus DEM GLO-30 is a 30 m global digital elevation model released through the Copernicus programme and derived from edited WorldDEM data. It is widely used in Google Earth Engine, Copernicus Data Space, and cloud-optimized workflows. The product is one of the strongest current global DEM baselines for terrain, hazards, hydrology, and environmental modelling.

source_type: official cloud catalog and provider portal

desc_en: Copernicus DEM GLO-90 is the 90 m version of the Copernicus global DEM family, preserving the edited WorldDEM-derived elevation surface at a lighter data volume. It is useful for global mapping, model preprocessing, and comparisons with 90 m products such as SRTMGL3. Access is available through Copernicus, GEE, and cloud mirrors.

source_type: official cloud catalog and provider portal

desc_en: AW3D30 is JAXA's global 30 m digital surface model derived from ALOS PRISM stereo observations. It provides an optical-stereo alternative to radar DEMs, with different viewing geometry and error characteristics. The dataset is commonly used for global terrain mapping, mountain analysis, and cross-validation among DEM products.

source_type: official provider portal and GEE mirror

name: MERIT DEM
provider: University of Tokyo / Yamazaki Lab
theme_raw: Multi-Error-Removed Improved-Terrain DEM
license: Creative Commons BY-NC 4.0 for research/education use
formats: GeoTIFF
access: project download with user registration/terms
doi_or_citation: Yamazaki et al. 2017, Geophysical Research Letters, MERIT DEM
time_span: released 2017; based on multiple global DEM inputs
notes: Error-reduced DEM removing major absolute bias, stripe noise, speckle noise, and tree-height bias from existing global DEMs.
desc_zh: MERIT DEM 是东京大学团队发布的多误差消除全球 DEM，重点校正绝对偏差、条带噪声、斑点噪声和树高偏差等问题。它在水文建模、洪水模拟和全球地形分析中影响很大，但许可证更偏科研教育使用。
desc_en: MERIT DEM is a multi-error-removed global DEM produced by the University of Tokyo team. It corrects major biases and noise patterns in existing global DEMs, including absolute bias, stripe noise, speckle noise, and tree-height bias. The product is especially influential in hydrological modelling, flood analysis, and global terrain studies, with access governed by research-oriented terms.
openness: free for research/education under stated terms
source_type: official project and peer-reviewed paper
evidence: MERIT DEM project page; Yamazaki et al. 2017 paper citation

desc_zh: FABDEM 是在 Copernicus DEM 基础上去除森林和建筑物高程偏差的全球近似裸地 DEM。它以开放数据仓库形式发布，尤其适合洪水风险、暴露度、城市与低地地形建模等需要裸地高程的应用。
desc_en: FABDEM is a global approximate bare-earth DEM generated by removing forest and building biases from Copernicus DEM. It is released through an open university data repository and is particularly valuable for flood-risk modelling, exposure assessment, urban terrain analysis, and other applications that require terrain elevation rather than surface height.
openness: open under CC BY 4.0
source_type: official repository and dataset paper
evidence: University of Bristol data repository record

desc_en: GMTED2010 is a USGS/NGA global multi-resolution terrain elevation dataset available at 30, 15, and 7.5 arc-second resolutions. It was developed as a successor to GTOPO30 and remains useful for coarse global mapping, climate and environmental modelling, and low-volume terrain workflows.

desc_en: HydroSHEDS provides SRTM-derived hydrologically conditioned elevation and drainage products designed for flow direction, accumulation, and basin analysis. This row captures the raster elevation and hydrological terrain product line rather than ordinary vector hydrography. It is a key global open source for hydrology-ready DEM workflows.

desc_en: ETOPO 2022 is NOAA NCEI's global topobathymetric relief model covering both land topography and ocean bathymetry. It is not a purely terrestrial DEM, but it is essential for continuous global elevation, coastal work, Earth-system modelling, and visualization. The dataset is distributed as U.S. public-domain data.

desc_en: The GEBCO global grid is a major open source for continuous bathymetric and topographic elevation. It is widely used for global relief, ocean depth, coastal analysis, and Earth visualization. In this DEM atlas it is treated as a topobathymetric adjacent core dataset rather than a purely terrestrial DEM.

time_span: Current maintained product; approximately 40,000 square kilometres are added each year; updated as needed according to previous-season laser scanning where significant surface changes are detected
notes: v076 refresh: expanded the original terse Finland 2 m row. NLS describes this as a 2 m x 2 m ground-surface elevation model based on airborne laser scanning with at least 0.5 points per square metre. It is the most accurate Finnish elevation model covering extensive areas, but NLS notes missing availability in some parts of the outer archipelago and eastern border. The product has two quality classes: class I averages 0.3 m elevation accuracy after automated LiDAR processing plus aerial-photo and manual control; class II varies from 0.3 m to 1 m and is produced faster from summer laser scanning without manual control, to be later replaced by class I. The 10 m Finland DEM row added in v075 remains distinct because it is a whole-country 10 m product partly derived from this 2 m source and partly from other topographic elevation data.
seen_in_passes: dem-v002-national-europe; v076 Finland NLS 2 m refresh with HVD/download/licence evidence
desc_zh: Finland Elevation Model 2 m 是芬兰国家土地测量局（NLS）发布的官方 2 米数字地形高程模型，基于点密度至少 0.5 点/平方米的航空激光雷达数据，采用 ETRS89 / TM35FIN 平面参考和 N2000 高程系统。NLS 说明该产品是芬兰覆盖大范围地区的最高精度高程模型，每年约新增 4 万平方公里；部分外群岛和东部边境区域可能暂无 2 米模型。产品分为质量等级 I 和 II：等级 I 平均高程精度约 0.3 米并经过航片与人工控制，等级 II 精度约 0.3-1 米、生产更快并会逐步由等级 I 替代。
desc_en: Finland Elevation Model 2 m is the National Land Survey of Finland’s official 2 m digital terrain model, based on airborne laser scanning with a minimum point density of 0.5 points per square metre and referenced to ETRS89 / TM35FIN and N2000 heights. NLS describes it as Finland’s most accurate elevation model for extensive areas, with about 40,000 square kilometres added annually; some outer archipelago and eastern border areas may lack coverage. The product is delivered in two quality classes: class I averages about 0.3 m elevation accuracy after LiDAR processing plus aerial-photo and manual control, while class II ranges from about 0.3 m to 1 m and is produced faster before later replacement by class I.

coverage: Conterminous United States (CONUS), with production begun in 2025 and tiles published as available; USGS program page states planned production for CONUS, Hawaii and Puerto Rico

formats: Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF (COG) 10 km x 10 km tiles; open-source GeoPackage spatial metadata; 1 m floating-point elevation values; NoData -999999; NAD83(2011) CONUS Albers Equal Area EPSG:6350; NAVD88 EPSG:5703 with GEOID18
access: USGS S1M product page, USGS DOI/data release landing page, The National Map Downloader, and USGS National Map data-access page
doi_or_citation: National Geospatial Technical Operations Center, Earth Resources Observation and Science Center, and National Geospatial Program, 2025, Seamless 1 meter Digital Elevation Models (DEMs) - USGS National Map 3DEP Downloadable Data Collection: U.S. Geological Survey data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/P13LJKFS.
time_span: Publication date 2025; beginning date of data 2024; ending date present; production began in 2025 and tiles are published as available
notes: Refreshed in v101 with DOI/data-release evidence and detailed product boundary. This row records the named USGS 3DEP Seamless 1-Meter DEM (S1M), not the broader project-based 1 m DEM holdings and not the GEE `USGS/3DEP/1m` project-tile collection. The USGS Science Data Catalog record states S1M was created to advance 3DNTM/next-generation 3DEP and 3DHP and is a Seamless 1-meter DEM for CONUS. It is produced from best available 3DEP Original Product Resolution source DEMs from one or more intersecting 3DEP data collection projects. Source DEMs are vertically aligned to NAVD88 EPSG:5703 with GEOID18 and projected horizontally to NAD83(2011) USA Contiguous Albers Equal Area Conic EPSG:6350; horizontal units and elevation values are meters. Large voids wider than 10 m are backfilled with 1/9 or 1/3 arc-second DEMs from the 3DEP repository, small voids are bilinearly interpolated, and multiple-project tiles can use simple, narrow or backfill blending. Each tile is 10 km x 10 km, delivered as Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF with spatial metadata GeoPackage. USGS states all 3DEP products are public domain. The USGS S1M product page explains that, unlike older project-based 1 m DEMs, S1M is designed to be seamless across project boundaries, reduce gaps/slivers, reduce water-surface mismatches from different collection dates, and avoid artifacts from varying coordinate systems/datums. Production began in 2025 with new tiles published as available.
seen_in_passes: dem-v003-product-splits, v101 GEE DEM tag follow-up and USGS 3DEP S1M DOI/data-release refresh
desc_zh: USGS 3DEP Seamless 1-Meter DEM (S1M) 是美国地质调查局 2025 年发布的无缝 1 米裸地数字高程模型产品，面向美国本土 CONUS，并按 10 km x 10 km 网格逐步发布。它把多个 3DEP 原始分辨率 DEM / 激光雷达项目统一到 NAD83(2011) CONUS Albers 和 NAVD88/GEOID18，高程值以米为单位，输出为 Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF，并配套 GeoPackage 空间元数据。与旧的项目级 1 米 DEM 不同，S1M 目标是在项目边界处减少空隙、错缝和不同基准/采集时期造成的不一致，是 3DEP 下一代无缝高程底图的重要新产品。USGS 记录说明 3DEP 产品为公共领域数据，可通过产品页、DOI 数据发布页和 The National Map 下载入口获取。
desc_en: USGS 3DEP Seamless 1-Meter DEM (S1M) is a 2025 USGS product providing a seamless 1 m bare-earth DEM for the conterminous United States, with 10 km by 10 km tiles published as production progresses. It harmonizes best-available 3DEP Original Product Resolution source DEMs from lidar projects to NAD83(2011) CONUS Albers and NAVD88/GEOID18, with meter units, Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF delivery, and GeoPackage spatial metadata. Unlike older project-based 1 m DEMs, S1M is designed to reduce gaps, slivers, water-surface mismatches, and datum/projection artifacts across project boundaries. USGS records the dataset as public domain and provides access through the product page, DOI data-release landing page and The National Map downloader.
access_status: open; USGS S1M product page, Science Data Catalog DOI/data-release record, The National Map downloader and National Map access page verified alive
openness: USGS Science Data Catalog states all 3DEP products are public domain; USGS product page and National Map page describe free/open public access through The National Map.
source_type: official USGS 3DEP product page, USGS Science Data Catalog DOI/data release, The National Map downloader/access page
evidence: USGS Science Data Catalog record USGS:4f34caac-f28f-4ea0-8d82-eafb2b8f9a5d states S1M is a Seamless 1-meter DEM for CONUS, created by NGTOC, EROS and NGP, publication date 2025, beginning date 2024 and ending date present, with DOI 10.5066/P13LJKFS. It describes 10 km x 10 km tiles, Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF output, open-source GeoPackage spatial metadata, 1 m floating-point values, NoData -999999, NAVD88 EPSG:5703 with GEOID18, NAD83(2011) CONUS Albers EPSG:6350, and use of best-available 3DEP OPR source DEMs, backfilling/interpolation/blending and metadata for those operations. It states all 3DEP products are public domain. The USGS S1M product page states S1M is a nationwide high-resolution elevation dataset merging trillions of lidar-derived terrain points into a continuous elevation surface; production began in 2025, new tiles are published as available, and production is planned for CONUS, Hawaii and Puerto Rico. The page explains S1M differs from existing project-based 1 m products by being seamless across project boundaries and reducing gaps, slivers, water mismatches and datum/projection artifacts. The National Map page documents that elevation DEMs and source lidar/IfSAR come from 3DEP and are accessed via data download, web map services and online viewer/downloader.

desc_en: Australian 5M LiDAR-derived DEM is a 5 m bare-earth elevation product from Geoscience Australia, compiled from 236 LiDAR surveys acquired between 2001 and 2015 across populated coastal zones, Murray-Darling floodplains, and surveyed population centres. Google Earth Engine exposes the dataset as AU/GA/AUSTRALIA_5M_DEM for cloud-based analysis.

notes: Accepted as the smoothed terrain-shape DEM-S product, distinct from the existing DEM-H hydrologically enforced row. GEE and GA metadata state that DEM-S removes vegetation features and applies adaptive smoothing to reduce SRTM noise for terrain attribute calculations.
desc_zh: DEM-S Australian Smoothed Digital Elevation Model 是 Geoscience Australia 基于 SRTM 制作的 1 弧秒约 30 米平滑数字高程模型。它通过自适应平滑降低噪声、改善地形形态表达，适合坡度、坡向和曲率等局地地形属性计算，与已入库的水文强化 DEM-H 是不同派生产品。
desc_en: DEM-S Australian Smoothed Digital Elevation Model is a 1 arc-second, roughly 30 m, SRTM-derived smoothed DEM from Geoscience Australia. It applies adaptive smoothing to reduce noise and improve terrain-shape representation for slope, aspect, and curvature calculations, and is distinct from the hydrologically enforced DEM-H product already catalogued.
openness: CC BY 4.0
source_type: Google Earth Engine catalog asset with official Geoscience Australia product record
evidence: GEE page confirms asset AU/GA/DEM_1SEC/v10/DEM-S, 30.92 m pixel size, CC BY 4.0 and smoothed SRTM-derived DEM description; GA record title confirms 1 second SRTM Level 2 Derived Smoothed Digital Elevation Model.

name: CPOM CryoSat-2 Antarctica 1km DEM (GEE CPOM/CryoSat2/ANTARCTICA_DEM)
provider: Centre for Polar Observation and Modelling (CPOM)
theme_raw: CryoSat-2 Antarctica 1km DEM
coverage: Antarctic ice sheet and ice shelves
license: Proprietary terms in GEE catalog; citation/DOI provided
formats: Google Earth Engine Image with elevation, data_composition, slope, z_smoothed and z_uncertainty bands
access: Google Earth Engine asset CPOM/CryoSat2/ANTARCTICA_DEM and The Cryosphere dataset paper/DOI
doi_or_citation: Slater et al., 2018, A new Digital Elevation Model of Antarctica derived from CryoSat-2 altimetry, The Cryosphere, DOI https://doi.org/10.5194/tc-2017-223
time_span: CryoSat-2 observations July 2010-July 2016
notes: Accepted as a GEE-accessible polar DEM with a clear paper-backed source and distinct sensor/processing lineage. It is not a duplicate of REMA, BedMachine Antarctica or EarthDEM because it is derived from CryoSat-2 radar-altimeter observations and includes uncertainty/data-composition bands. Marked with proprietary GEE terms rather than fully open download.

desc_en: CPOM CryoSat-2 Antarctica 1km DEM is a digital elevation model of the Antarctic ice sheet and ice shelves derived from CryoSat-2 radar-altimeter observations collected between July 2010 and July 2016. notes: The official Defra Data Services page describes the LIDAR Composite DSM as a raster elevation model covering about 99% of England at 1 m spatial resolution. The DSM is produced from the last or only laser pulse returned to the sensor and includes surface objects such as vehicles, buildings, and vegetation as well as terrain. The 2022 composite merges Environment Agency Time Stamped archive and National LIDAR Programme surveys, using the newest and best-resolution data where repeats exist. Data are downloadable as GeoTIFF rasters in 5 km OS National Grid tiles, in metres relative to Ordnance Survey Newlyn using OSTN15, with individual-survey vertical accuracy about +/-15 cm RMSE. This row complements the existing bare-earth Environment Agency LIDAR Composite DTM 1m row.
desc_zh: Environment Agency LIDAR Composite DSM 1m 是英格兰环境署发布的 1 米数字表面模型，覆盖约 99% 的英格兰。它由 2000-2022 年间的时间戳 LiDAR 档案和国家 LiDAR 计划数据合成，保留建筑、植被、车辆等表面目标高度，可通过 Defra/EA 下载门户获取 GeoTIFF 瓦片，并在 Google Earth Engine 中作为 DTM/DSM 复合影像使用。
desc_en: Environment Agency LIDAR Composite DSM 1m is a 1 m digital surface model covering approximately 99% of England. It is produced from Environment Agency Time Stamped archive and National LIDAR Programme surveys collected between 2000 and 2022, retaining surface objects such as buildings, vegetation, and vehicles as well as terrain. The product is distributed as GeoTIFF tiles through Defra/EA services and is also available in Google Earth Engine as part of the 2022 England 1 m DTM/DSM composite.
openness: open under Open Government Licence v3.0 with attribution
source_type: official data portal/download service and GEE cloud analysis mirror
evidence: Defra Data Services dataset 9ba4d5ac-d596-445a-9056-dae3ddec0178 title LIDAR Composite Digital Surface Model (DSM) - 1m; summary says raster elevation model covering ~99% of England at 1m resolution, produced from last/only laser pulse and including surface objects; source surveys 2000-06-06 to 2022-04-02; downloadable as GeoTIFF rasters in 5km OS National Grid tiles; no public access constraints; licence Open Government Licence; vertical accuracy +/-15cm RMSE. GEE catalog UK_EA_ENGLAND_1M_TERRAIN_2022 lists 1m England composite DTM/DSM, three bands including first return DSM and last return DSM, asset UK/EA/ENGLAND_1M_TERRAIN/2022, same date range, producer UK Environment Agency, and free use with attribution.
feature_count: England national DSM tile collection; GEE image has DTM, first-return DSM, and last-return DSM bands

time_span: National LIDAR Programme source data from 2016-11-11 to 2022-05-05; creation date 2023-02-08; revision date 2023-12-15
notes: The official Defra Data Services page describes the first-return DSM as a raster elevation model covering about 99% of England at 1 m spatial resolution. It is produced from the first or only laser pulse returned to the sensor and includes heights of objects such as vehicles, buildings, and vegetation, plus terrain where the first/only return is ground. It is derived from National LIDAR Programme surveys captured between 11 November 2016 and 5 May 2022, merged with feathering along overlaps. The page explicitly says it will not match the coverage or extent of the last-return DSM because the last-return product also uses Time Series surveys. This row is distinct from v037 last/only return DSM and the existing DTM row.
desc_zh: Environment Agency LIDAR Composite First Return DSM 1m 是英格兰环境署发布的 1 米首回波数字表面模型，主要由 2016-2022 年国家 LiDAR 计划数据合成。它记录激光首次或唯一回波得到的表面高度，突出植被冠层、建筑物和其他最高可见表面，覆盖约 99% 的英格兰，但与常规 last-return DSM 的覆盖范围并不完全一致。
desc_en: Environment Agency LIDAR Composite First Return DSM 1m is a 1 m first-return digital surface model for England. It is derived from National LIDAR Programme surveys collected from 2016 to 2022 and records the first or only laser return, emphasizing canopy, buildings, vehicles, and other upper visible surfaces. The product covers approximately 99% of England but does not exactly match the last-return DSM extent, making it a distinct surface-height dataset.

time_span: PNOA-LiDAR first coverage; catalog option indicates MDS05 2008-2015; datos.gob modified 2020-01-01
notes: MDS05 is a 5 m digital surface model derived from first-coverage PNOA-LiDAR classified point clouds. CNIG states that it is interpolated from terrain, vegetation (low, medium, high), and building classes, with ETRS89 for mainland/Balearic/Ceuta/Melilla and REGCAN95 for Canary Islands, UTM projection by sheet, orthometric heights, MTN50 sheet downloads, and current COG format. The PNOA documentation describes MDS as the highest surface over terrain, natural objects, or artificial objects and says MDS generation uses first returns from terrain, vegetation, and building classes. datos.gob provides additional open-catalog evidence but appears older, describing ASCII ASC distribution and noting no MDS for Ceuta, Melilla, or Gibraltar.
desc_zh: Spain MDS05 是西班牙 IGN/CNIG 发布的 5 米数字表面模型，基于 PNOA-LiDAR 第一覆盖的分类点云生成，包含地面、植被和建筑物等最高表面信息。当前 CNIG 下载中心按 MTN50 图幅提供下载，并标注 COG 格式；该产品与已有 MDT02/MDT05 裸地地形模型不同，更适合城市表面、植被/建筑高度背景、可视性和表面粗糙度分析。
desc_en: Spain MDS05 is a 5 m digital surface model from IGN/CNIG derived from first-coverage PNOA-LiDAR classified point clouds. It represents the highest surface using terrain, vegetation, and building classes and is distributed through the CNIG Download Center by MTN50 sheet, with current product metadata indicating COG format. It complements the existing Spanish MDT terrain-model rows by representing surface objects rather than bare-earth terrain.
openness: open with attribution under IGN/CNIG terms compatible with CC-BY 4.0
source_type: official national download portal, national open-data catalog, and PNOA product documentation
evidence: CNIG Download Center page title Modelo Digital de Superficies MDS05 - 1ª cobertura; description says 5 m grid surface model obtained from terrain, vegetation, and building classes of first-coverage PNOA LiDAR; reference systems ETRS89/REGCAN95, UTM, orthometric heights; MTN50 download units; format COG; license compatible with CC-BY 4.0 with attribution; page lists 1,180 files. PNOA MDS documentation defines MDS as highest surface over terrain, natural vegetation, or artificial objects and generation from first returns of classes 2-6. datos.gob catalog e00125901-spaignmds confirms national 5 m surface model, PNOA-LiDAR first coverage lineage, creator CNIG, and license conditions URL.
feature_count: 1,180 CNIG MTN50 sheet files listed on the current Download Center page

notes: MNS LiDAR HD is the IGN digital surface model derived from classified LiDAR HD point clouds. data.gouv.fr describes a regular grid providing heights of terrain and above-ground elements such as vegetation, buildings, and engineering structures, derived from ground, water, low/medium/high vegetation, building, and bridge-deck classes. It is distributed as 1 km x 1 km GeoTIFF tiles at 50 cm resolution; a 5 m version is planned. The direct diffusion-lidarhd.ign.fr/mnx/ directory returned 503 in this environment during v040 probing, so the verified URL list uses stable data.gouv and GeoPlateforme API/service endpoints rather than the transient directory.
desc_zh: France MNS LiDAR HD 是法国 IGN 基于国家 LiDAR HD 计划分类点云生成的高分辨率数字表面模型，表达地面以及植被、建筑物、桥面等地上要素高度。data.gouv.fr 说明该产品以 1 km x 1 km GeoTIFF 瓦片发布，网格间距 50 厘米，并计划到 2026 年覆盖法国本土和除圭亚那外的海外省区，适合城市表面、植被结构、基础设施和 DSM/DTM 对比分析。
desc_en: France MNS LiDAR HD is IGN France's high-resolution digital surface model derived from classified national LiDAR HD point clouds. It represents terrain and above-ground objects such as vegetation, buildings, and bridge decks, and is distributed as 1 km by 1 km GeoTIFF tiles at 50 cm spacing. The product is intended to cover metropolitan France and overseas departments/regions except Guyane by 2026, supporting surface-height, urban, vegetation, infrastructure, and DSM/DTM comparison workflows.
openness: open under Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0
source_type: official national open-data catalog and GeoPlateforme services/API

time_span: Temporal coverage 2020-01-01 to 2026-01-01; data.gouv last update 2026-06-26; departmental updates every 3-4 years following BD ORTHO acquisition program
notes: MNS Correl is an IGN digital surface model at 50 cm spacing derived from BD ORTHO aerial imagery by image correlation, not from LiDAR. It describes the shape of ground and above-ground surface objects such as vegetation and buildings. It covers French metropolitan and overseas territories except French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Wallis and Futuna, with nodata over ZICAD restricted aerial data capture zones. This row is separate from France MNS LiDAR HD because the production method and source data differ, and separate from RGE ALTI because it is a DSM rather than a bare-earth terrain product.
desc_zh: France MNS Correl 是法国 IGN 发布的 50 厘米数字表面模型，基于 BD ORTHO 航空影像通过影像相关方法生成，而不是 LiDAR 点云。它描述地面及植被、建筑等地上表面形态，覆盖法国本土和多数海外领地（不含法属波利尼西亚、新喀里多尼亚、瓦利斯和富图纳），适合城市三维、林业、太阳能地籍、可视性和 DSM/DTM 对比分析。
desc_en: France MNS Correl is IGN France's 50 cm digital surface model produced from BD ORTHO aerial imagery using image correlation. It represents ground and above-ground surface features such as vegetation and buildings across metropolitan France and most overseas territories, excluding French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Wallis and Futuna, with nodata in restricted ZICAD areas. It complements MNS LiDAR HD by using a different source and production method, and complements RGE ALTI as a DSM rather than a bare-earth terrain model.

API lists GeoPlateforme WMS/WMTS resources ELEVATION.ELEVATIONGRIDCOVERAGE.HIGHRES.MNS and SHADOW.

desc_zh: Poland Digital Surface Model / NMPT 是波兰 Geoportal.gov.pl 发布的国家数字表面模型，表达地形以及建筑物、树木、桥梁、立交和其他基础设施等高出地面的对象。官方页面说明城市区域网格约 0.5 米，其他区域约 1 米，数据免费并可任意用途使用，可通过 Geoportal 下载图幅、WFS 索引和 WCS 工具获取。
desc_en: Poland Digital Surface Model / NMPT is the national digital surface model distributed through Geoportal.gov.pl. It represents terrain together with above-ground objects such as buildings, trees, bridges, overpasses, and infrastructure, with a 0.5 m mesh in urban areas and a 1.0 m mesh elsewhere. The official page states that the data are free of charge and may be used for any purpose, with downloads through Geoportal layers and WFS/WCS workflows.

coverage: Czech Republic national coverage, provided as DMP 1G in S-JTSK/Bpv; official metadata describes coverage of the territory including buildings and vegetation cover
license: No charges; Creative Commons CC BY 4.0 License; according to Regulation No. 31/1995 Sb.

time_span: Source airborne laser scanning acquired across the Czech Republic between 2009 and 2013; official page notes DMP 1G will no longer be provided from 2027-01-01 and is being replaced by DMP OK
notes: DMP 1G is a first-generation national digital surface model distinct from the existing DMR 5G bare-earth terrain model row. Official metadata states that the model represents the territory including buildings and vegetation cover as an irregular network of height points with full planimetric and altimetric accuracy. The product page reports RMSE height accuracy of about 0.4 m for clearly delineated objects such as buildings and 0.7 m for less clearly delineated objects such as forests and vegetation. Data were created from airborne laser scanning from 2009 to 2013. The official page warns that DMP 1G is outdated in most areas and will stop being provided from 2027-01-01; DMP OK is the replacement product and should be tracked in a later cycle.
desc_zh: Czech Republic DMP 1G 是捷克测绘与地籍机构 ČÚZK 发布的第一代国家数字表面模型，覆盖捷克全国，表达地表以及建筑物、植被等地物的高度。官方页面说明数据源自 2009-2013 年航空激光扫描，LAZ/ATOM 等方式可获取，使用条件为免费且采用 CC BY 4.0；页面同时提示该产品多数区域已过时，并将在 2027 年 1 月 1 日后由 DMP OK 继续替代提供。
desc_en: Czech Republic DMP 1G is the first-generation national digital surface model published by ČÚZK. It represents the Czech territory including buildings and vegetation cover, was created from airborne laser scanning acquired between 2009 and 2013, and is accessible through the ČÚZK Geoportal with LAZ/ATOM download routes. The official product page lists no charges and CC BY 4.0 terms, while noting that DMP 1G is being replaced by DMP OK and will stop being provided from 2027-01-01.
openness: free access under CC BY 4.0 according to ČÚZK product and metadata pages
source_type: official national geoportal product page, metadata record, ATOM feed, and national open-data catalogue record
evidence: ČÚZK product page identifies ZABAGED® - Altimetry - DMP 1G as the Digital Surface Model of the Czech Republic of the 1st generation in S-JTSK/Bpv, with no charges, CC BY 4.0, LAZ export format, web map view, ATOM service, export of current extent, and pre-defined file downloads. Full metadata states the model represents territory including buildings and vegetation cover as an irregular network of height points, was created from airborne laser scanning between 2009 and 2013, and is registered in the National Open Data Catalogue. The page notes DMP 1G will no longer be provided from 2027-01-01 and DMP OK will replace it.
scale: national DSM; official accuracy note reports about 0.4 m RMSE for clearly delineated objects and 0.7 m for forests/vegetation
file_size: official product list shows a 7.2 GB full download route

name: Czech Republic DMP OK Digital Surface Model from Image Correlation
provider: Czech Office for Surveying, Mapping and Cadastre (ČÚZK)
theme_raw: ZABAGED® - Altimetry - DMP OK / Digital surface model of the Czech Republic from image correlation
coverage: Czech Republic national coverage in S-JTSK/Bpv; official product represents terrain including natural and human-made objects such as vegetation cover and buildings
license: No charges; Creative Commons CC BY 4.0 License; according to Regulation No. 31/1995 Sb.

access: ČÚZK Geoportal product page and full metadata; ATOM services for S-JTSK LAZ and S-JTSK TIFF; National Open Data Catalogue / Geoportal.gov.cz metadata record
doi_or_citation: ČÚZK metadata identifier CZ-CUZK-DMPOK; ZABAGED® - Altimetry - Digital surface model of the Czech Republic from image correlation
time_span: First generated from aerial survey photographs acquired in 2024; official page says update follows the Orthophoto of the Czech Republic cycle, currently a two-year cycle
notes: DMP OK is the newer ČÚZK digital surface model derived by image correlation and is distinct from DMP 1G, which was derived from 2009-2013 airborne laser scanning and is being phased out. The official product page describes terrain plus all natural and human-made objects, with natural objects as vegetation cover and human-made objects as buildings. It is available as a point cloud arranged in a regular 0.2 x 0.2 m grid and as a 0.5 x 0.5 m raster derived by interpolation from the point cloud. It is a high-priority follow-up to v043 because ČÚZK says DMP OK continues provision after DMP 1G stops being provided from 2027-01-01. The S-JTSK LAZ and TIFF ATOM feeds were reachable in v044; ETRS89 ATOM feeds are also advertised and can be checked in a later enrichment pass.
desc_zh: Czech Republic DMP OK 是捷克 ČÚZK 发布的新一代数字表面模型，由正射影像生产体系中的航测量测影像通过影像相关生成。它表达地形及植被、建筑物等自然和人工地物，官方页面说明自 2024 年数据首次生成，并按 Ortofoto ČR 约两年周期更新；数据以 0.2 米规则网格点云和由点云插值得到的 0.5 米栅格提供，LAZ 与 TIFF ATOM 下载服务可访问，使用条件为免费和 CC BY 4.0。
desc_en: Czech Republic DMP OK is ČÚZK's newer national digital surface model derived from image correlation of aerial survey photographs acquired for Ortofoto ČR. It represents terrain together with natural and human-made objects such as vegetation cover and buildings, with production starting from 2024 data and updates tied to the roughly two-year orthophoto cycle. The product is available as a 0.2 m regular-grid point cloud and a 0.5 m interpolated raster through LAZ and TIFF ATOM services under no-charge CC BY 4.0 terms.
openness: free access under CC BY 4.0 according to ČÚZK product and metadata pages
source_type: official national geoportal product page, metadata record, ATOM feeds, and national open-data catalogue record
evidence: ČÚZK product page identifies ZABAGED® - Altimetry - Digital surface model of the Czech Republic from image correlation in S-JTSK/Bpv. It states no charges, CC BY 4.0, LAZ and TIFF export formats, WMS/view service, ATOM services, current-extent export, and pre-defined downloads. The product description says the DSM represents terrain including natural and human-made objects, with vegetation cover and buildings specifically named; it was first generated from 2024 aerial survey photographs and is updated with the Orthophoto of the Czech Republic cycle, currently two years. S-JTSK LAZ and TIFF ATOM feeds returned HTTP 200 in v044 verification.
scale: 0.2 m regular-grid point cloud and 0.5 m raster grid

notes: DOM20 is distinct from the existing Bavaria DGM1 row: DGM1 is a terrain model without vegetation and buildings, while DOM20 is a surface model including objects such as vegetation and buildings. desc_zh: Bavaria DOM20 是巴伐利亚测绘主管部门发布的 20 厘米数字表面模型，覆盖德国巴伐利亚州，表达地表以及植被、建筑物等地物。官方 OpenData 产品清单列明许可证为 CC BY 4.0，提供 GeoTIFF、1 km x 1 km 切片、单项下载和 Metalink 批量下载；LDBV 产品页说明其为 0.2 米地面像元、全州可用，并随巴伐利亚航摄周期更新。
desc_en: Bavaria DOM20 is the 20 cm digital surface model distributed by the Bavarian surveying administration for the German state of Bavaria. It represents the earth surface together with objects such as vegetation and buildings, and is offered as GeoTIFF in 1 km x 1 km tiles through OpenData download and Metalink workflows. The official OpenData catalogue lists CC BY 4.0 terms, while the LDBV product page documents statewide availability, 0.2 m ground pixel size, DHHN2016/ETRS89 reference information, and update linkage to Bavaria's aerial survey cycle.
openness: CC BY 4.0 in the Bavarian OpenData product catalogue
source_type: official regional open-data catalogue, product JSON, product information page, and KML/Metalink download indexes
evidence: Bavarian OpenData JSON product `dom20` identifies `Digitales Oberflächenmodell 20cm (DOM20)`, describes it as showing the earth surface including objects such as vegetation and buildings in grid form, lists license `CC BY 4.0`, transfer modes DOWNLOAD and MASSENDOWNLOAD, GeoTIFF format, 1 km x 1 km tiles, UTM32 EPSG:25832, and approximately 30-50 MB per tile. The LDBV DOM product page states that the DOM shows the earth surface and objects such as vegetation and buildings, is a raster with 0.2 m ground pixel size, is available statewide for Bavaria, is delivered as GeoTIFF in 1-km² tiles, has DHHN2016 height reference and ETRS89 datum, and reports horizontal/vertical accuracy of 0.4-0.6 m. Municipality and district KML indexes expose Metalink download links.

feature_count: statewide Bavaria DOM20 tile collection; KML indexes list municipality/district Metalink downloads
file_size: approximately 30-50 MB per 1 km tile; municipality downloads can reach about 24 GB and district downloads about 126 GB according to product JSON

desc_en: North Rhine-Westphalia DOM1 is the 1 m digital surface model distributed by Geobasis NRW for the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia. It models the heights and shapes of the earth surface together with objects such as vegetation and structures, using airborne laser scanning measurements as its source. Open.NRW/CKAN provides a GeoTIFF download directory and WCS service under Datenlizenz Deutschland Zero 2.0, with official documentation stating free online use without restrictions or conditions.
openness: Datenlizenz Deutschland Zero 2.0; official WCS/product text states use without restrictions or conditions
source_type: official state open-data portal, CKAN API, Geobasis NRW product documentation, open download directory, and OGC WCS service
evidence: Open.NRW dataset dd2899b6-a02f-4d28-bed3-54322d315f98 and CKAN package_show identify `Digitales Oberflächenmodell 1 NW`, license `Datenlizenz Deutschland – Zero – Version 2.0`, GeoTIFF download URL `https://www.opengeodata.nrw.de/produkte/geobasis/hm/dom1_tiff/dom1_tiff/`, and WCS URL `https://www.wcs.nrw.de/geobasis/wcs_nw_dom?REQUEST=GetCapabilities&SERVICE=WCS`. The CKAN notes describe DOM as a regular-grid model of the heights/forms of the earth surface and objects such as vegetation and structures, DOM1 grid spacing of one meter, and airborne laser scanning 3D measurements as the source. Bezirksregierung Köln pages state Open Data provision since 2023-05-17, free online procedures without restrictions/conditions, 1 km x 1 km raster tiles, GeoTIFF format, and WCS access.

feature_count: statewide NRW DOM1 tile collection plus WCS coverage service

notes: This row adds Niedersachsen/Lower Saxony, which was absent from v046. DOM1 is a digital surface model derived from ALS point clouds, not a bare-earth terrain model. LGLN states that the DOM describes the earth surface together with non-temporary objects, with 1 m grid spacing, 1 x 1 km² tiles, pixel centers at 0.5 m positions, and height accuracy for solid surfaces without vegetation of <= 30 cm. The COG is 32-bit float, LZW-compressed, and uses -9999 for NoData. The OpenGeoData item lists WMS, STAC API, mass-download GeoJSON, EPSG:25832 for horizontal reference and EPSG:7837/DHHN2016 for heights. NUMIS and the LGLN product page state CC BY 4.0 terms and the LGLN attribution requirement.
desc_zh: Lower Saxony DOM1 是德国下萨克森州 LGLN 发布的 1 米数字表面模型，基于全州自 2019 年以来可用的航空激光扫描点云生成。数据以 1 x 1 km² 切片的 Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF 提供，并通过 STAC API、WMS 和 GeoJSON 批量下载索引访问；官方元数据说明其表达地表及非临时对象，固定无植被表面的高程精度不超过约 30 厘米，许可证为 CC BY 4.0。
desc_en: Lower Saxony DOM1 is the 1 m digital surface model published by LGLN for Niedersachsen, Germany. It is derived from statewide airborne laser scanning point clouds available since 2019 and is distributed as 1 x 1 km² Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF tiles with STAC API, WMS, and mass-download GeoJSON access. Official metadata describes the surface together with non-temporary objects, reports height accuracy of <= 30 cm for solid unvegetated surfaces, and uses CC BY 4.0 terms.
openness: CC BY 4.0 with LGLN attribution according to NUMIS and LGLN OpenGeoData item
source_type: official state open-data hub, ArcGIS REST metadata, NUMIS catalogue record, product page, STAC API, WMS, and mass-download index
evidence: ArcGIS REST item `a26d7889d054418db27d11501ab74f8e` identifies `Digitales Oberflächenmodell (DOM1)`, tags it as Niedersachsen/OpenData/DOM, and lists access routes including WMS `https://opendata.lgln.niedersachsen.de/doorman/noauth/dom_wms`, STAC API `https://dom.stac.lgln.niedersachsen.de`, OpenAPI description, and mass-download GeoJSON `https://arcgis-geojson.s3.eu-de.cloud-object-storage.appdomain.cloud/dom1/lgln-opengeodata-dom1.geojson`. It states data format Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, tile size 1 x 1 km², 1 m resolution, file size under 5 MB per tile, EPSG:25832 horizontal CRS, EPSG:7837/DHHN2016 heights, and height accuracy <= 30 cm for fixed surfaces without vegetation. NUMIS and the LGLN product page repeat DOM1/1 m/COG/ALS/CC BY 4.0 evidence and identify the dataset as INSPIRE Open Data / HVD.

feature_count: statewide Lower Saxony DOM1 COG tile collection indexed by STAC and mass-download GeoJSON
file_size: less than 5 MB per tile according to ArcGIS REST item description

time_span: Represents the time of aerial image acquisition; ArcGIS REST item modified 2026-05-31 in inspected metadata; product page describes image-based DSM from oriented aerial imagery
notes: bDOM20 is distinct from the v047 Lower Saxony DOM1 row. DOM1 is derived from airborne laser scanning and has 1 m grid spacing, while bDOM20 is derived from image correlation / dense image matching of oriented aerial photographs and has 0.2 m grid spacing. The ArcGIS REST item states that image-based digital surface models represent the earth surface and objects such as vegetation and buildings at the time of aerial-image acquisition. It also states that a regular grid is produced from the correlation and modeling of resulting point clouds, that pixels may carry attributes such as RGBI color information, and that another attribute indicates whether a height value was computed from image correlation or synthetically generated. Access is through STAC API, mass-download GeoJSON, and a public ArcGIS Feature Service index.
desc_zh: Lower Saxony bDOM20 是德国下萨克森州 LGLN 发布的 20 厘米图像相关数字表面模型。它由定向航摄影像的相关匹配和点云建模生成，表达影像获取时刻的地表以及植被、建筑物等对象；官方 OpenGeoData item 提供 STAC API、GeoJSON 批量下载索引和 ArcGIS Feature Service，许可证为 CC BY 4.0。
desc_en: Lower Saxony bDOM20 is LGLN's 20 cm image-based digital surface model for Niedersachsen, Germany. It is generated from correlation / dense image matching of oriented aerial photographs and models the earth surface plus objects such as vegetation and buildings at the time of image acquisition. The official OpenGeoData item provides STAC API, mass-download GeoJSON, and ArcGIS Feature Service access under CC BY 4.0 terms.

The same description states bDOM20 has uniform 0.2 m grid spacing and follows the AdV product and quality standard for image-based digital surface models. desc_zh: Hamburg DGM1 是德国汉堡市州 LGV 发布的 1 米数字地形/高程模型，覆盖自由汉萨城汉堡。数据由航空激光扫描或摄影测量产品派生，来源包括 2010、2020 和 2022 年全域 3D 激光扫描飞行；官方透明门户提供 CKAN 元数据、WMS 服务和全市 ASCII XYZ ZIP 下载，许可证为德国数据署名许可 DL-DE BY 2.0。
desc_en: Hamburg DGM1 is the 1 m digital terrain/elevation model published by Hamburg's Landesbetrieb Geoinformation und Vermessung for the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg. It is derived from airborne laser scanning or photogrammetric products, with source data from citywide 3D laser scan flights in 2010, 2020, and 2022. The Hamburg Transparency Portal provides CKAN/DCAT metadata, WMS access, and full-city ASCII XYZ ZIP downloads under the German DL-DE BY 2.0 attribution license.

coverage: Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg, Germany, excluding the Hamburg Wadden Sea area according to the transparency portal description
license: Datenlizenz Deutschland - Namensnennung - Version 2.0 (dl-de/by-2-0)
formats: ASCII full-city downloads for 2018 and 2020; CKAN/DCAT metadata XML/JSON/RDF; MetaVer HTML metadata
access: Hamburg Transparency Portal dataset page and CKAN API, DCAT/RDF XML metadata, archived ASCII download resources, and MetaVer metadata page
doi_or_citation: Hamburg Transparency Portal package digitales-hoehenmodell-hamburg-bdom / dataset 59e8316a-2fce-44f9-89a9-6a35e7e2bb2a; older package digitales-hohenmodell-hamburg-bdom / dataset 66f003fa-e2b7-49c0-8a89-4c3f524a126b
time_span: Photogrammetric image flight reference: spring 2020; package publication 2021-11-26; resources include 2018 and 2020 bDOM ASCII downloads
notes: Hamburg bDOM is distinct from the v049 Hamburg DGM1 terrain model. DGM1 is a bare-earth terrain/elevation model, while bDOM is an image-based digital surface model that represents the earth surface and objects such as structures at the time of aerial-image acquisition. The transparency portal describes bDOM as derived by image correlation, usually Dense Image Matching, from oriented digital aerial images and as an intermediate product in the TrueDOP production process. The newer package `digitales-hoehenmodell-hamburg-bdom` is used as canonical; older spelling variant `digitales-hohenmodell-hamburg-bdom` was treated as a historical package variant for the same product. Although resource names contain `dom1_xyz`, the title and description identify the product as bDOM. Downloads are large ASCII resources, not WMS services in the inspected package.
desc_zh: Hamburg bDOM 是汉堡 LGV 发布的图像相关数字表面模型，覆盖自由汉萨城汉堡（不含汉堡瓦登海区域）。它由定向数字航摄影像通过影像相关 / Dense Image Matching 推导，表达摄影测量飞行时刻的地表及建筑等对象，是 TrueDOP 生产流程中的中间产品；透明门户提供 2018 和 2020 年全市 ASCII 下载，许可证为 DL-DE BY 2.0。
desc_en: Hamburg bDOM is the image-based digital surface model published by Hamburg LGV for the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg, excluding the Hamburg Wadden Sea area. It is derived from oriented digital aerial imagery by image correlation, typically Dense Image Matching, and represents the earth surface plus objects such as structures at the time of aerial image acquisition. The Transparency Portal provides full-city ASCII downloads for 2018 and 2020 under the DL-DE BY 2.0 attribution license.
openness: DL-DE BY 2.0 open data license with attribution to Freie und Hansestadt Hamburg, Landesbetrieb Geoinformation und Vermessung (LGV)
source_type: official city-state transparency/open-data portal, CKAN API, DCAT/RDF metadata, direct archive downloads, and MetaVer metadata
evidence: Hamburg Transparency Portal package `digitales-hoehenmodell-hamburg-bdom` and CKAN package_show identify `Digitales Höhenmodell Hamburg - bDOM`, license `Datenlizenz Deutschland - Namensnennung - 2.0`, provider LGV, and resources `Download Digitales Höhenmodel Hamburg - bDOM, 2018` and `2020` in ASCII format. The description states that bDOM is a derived, area-wide digital surface model with 1 m grid spacing for Hamburg excluding the Wadden Sea, that it describes the earth surface and objects such as structures as regular georeferenced height points at the time of a photogrammetric image flight (spring 2020), and that it is generated by image correlation / Dense Image Matching from oriented digital aerial images. The portal page and XML metadata repeat ETRS89_UTM32 / EPSG:25832 and DE_DHHN2016_NH reference information plus DL-DE BY 2.0 licensing.
feature_count: full Hamburg bDOM ASCII surface-model downloads for 2018 and 2020 in the inspected package
file_size: approximately 3.3 GB for 2018 ASCII and 3.5 GB for 2020 ASCII

The Berlin.de page says the underlying height information was acquired by airborne laser scanning in the first quarter of 2021 and updated photogrammetrically. The CSW dataset record adds stereoscopic image-flight updates in 03/2022, 04/2023, 03/2025, and 08/2025. Use is governed by Datenlizenz Deutschland Zero 2.0.
desc_zh: Berlin ATKIS DGM1 是德国柏林 GDI/Senatsverwaltung 发布的 1 米数字地形模型，表达不含植被、建筑等自然和人工对象的裸地地表。官方页面和 CSW 元数据说明数据以 2 km x 2 km 图幅组织，覆盖柏林并向勃兰登堡延伸约 250 米缓冲区，基础高程由 2021 年第一季度航空激光扫描获取，并通过 2022、2023、2025 年影像飞行立体测图更新；可通过 ATOM 下载和 WMS 服务访问，许可证为 DL-DE Zero 2.0。
desc_en: Berlin ATKIS DGM1 is the 1 m digital terrain model published by GDI Berlin / the Senate Department for Urban Development, Building and Housing. It represents the bare earth surface without natural objects such as vegetation or artificial objects such as buildings, organized in 2 km x 2 km sheets and extending about 250 m into Brandenburg. Official records describe airborne laser scanning acquisition in early 2021 and photogrammetric/stereoscopic updates from 2022, 2023, and 2025, with access through ATOM download and WMS services under DL-DE Zero 2.0.
openness: DL-DE Zero 2.0; official metadata states no access restrictions and unrestricted use under the German Zero license
source_type: official city-state product page, CSW catalogue records, ATOM download feed, WMS service, and PDF documentation
evidence: Berlin.de product page states ATKIS DGM contains height information as a 1 m regular raster, based on airborne laser scanning in Q1 2021 and photogrammetric updates, using ETRS89/UTM Zone 33N (EPSG:25833) and DHHN2016 (EPSG:7837), with DGM1 datasets in 2 km x 2 km sheets and an ATOM download service. GDI Berlin CSW dataset record `f0e8ff09-2887-3446-9c53-81dbc45af03c` states that DGM describes the earth surface without vegetation or buildings, has 1 m grid spacing in 2 km x 2 km sheet cuts, extends about 250 m into Brandenburg, and provides ATOM, WMS, PDF, and product-page links. notes: Accepted as the Saarland/LVGL-SL DGM1 terrain-model entry. The SIG-GR Geodaten Saarland table states that DGM1 describes the spatial form of the earth surface in 3D coordinates with 1 m grid spacing, uses ETRS89_UTM32 and DE_DHHN2016_NH, is provided as ASCII XYZ, and is OpenData under DL-DE BY 2.0. Geoportal Saarland WMS GetCapabilities for layer/service ID 49932 states `WMS SL Digitale Geländemodelle`, describes DGM as numeric regular-grid terrain-height/shape models of the earth surface without buildings/bridges/vegetation, identifies Landesamt für Vermessung, Geoinformation und Landentwicklung Saarland as provider, lists no limitations/noLimitations and opendata keywords, and exposes a `Shaded Relief (2025)` layer derived from current laser data.

desc_zh: Thuringia Digital Terrain Models (DGM) 是德国图林根州 TLBG/GDI-Th 发布的数字地形模型集合。稳定的 GeoMIS 元数据记录说明该数据集描述地表地形起伏，可按不同精度和栅格/点间距区分，格式为坐标三元组 ASCII（右值、高值、高程），参考系统为 EPSG:25832，开放许可为 DL-DE BY 2.0，并提供 WMS TH DGM 与 Geoportal Thüringen 高程数据下载区资源；TLBG 资料说明 DGM/DOM/LoD2 通过航空激光扫描周期性更新。
desc_en: Thuringia Digital Terrain Models (DGM) is the official TLBG / GDI-Th terrain-model collection for the German state of Thuringia. The stable GeoMIS metadata record describes DGM as terrain-relief models represented by regular or irregular point grids and differentiated by accuracy and grid spacing. It lists ASCII coordinate triples, EPSG:25832, DL-DE BY 2.0 licensing, WMS TH DGM and Geoportal Thüringen height-data download resources, while TLBG actuality documents describe cyclic airborne laser-scanning acquisition and updates for DGM, DOM, and LoD2.
openness: DL-DE BY 2.0; GeoMIS metadata states no public access restrictions and requires attribution to the data-holding/providing body and year of access, e.g. GDI-Th (2021)
source_type: official state GeoMIS metadata, GDI-Th WMS service, Geoportal open-data download pages, TLBG product/actuality documentation, and official license page
evidence: GeoMIS.Th record `14418d25-fcd7-4a3f-99a9-e3059a2772af` identifies `Digitales Geländemodell`, alternate title `Digitale Geländemodelle`, ongoing status, continual updates, topic category Elevation, opendata and airborne-laserscanning keywords, no access restrictions, DL-DE BY 2.0 licensing with attribution `© GDI-Th`, distance 2 m, EPSG:25832, and distribution format `Koordinatentripel (Rechtswert, Hochwert, Höhe)` in ASCII format. Associated online resources include `DGM5` and `DGM2` WMS/hillshade resources and `Download Offene Geodaten - Höhendaten`. The record statement says TLBG has used Airborne Laserscanning since 1996 for acquisition. Geoportal Thüringen height-data and service pages provide official download/service context, and TLBG 2020-2025 actuality PDFs document cyclic airborne laser-scanning acquisition for DGM/DOM/LoD2.
scale: GeoMIS metadata distance 2 m; DGM family includes multiple accuracy/grid-spacing variants such as DGM2/DGM5 service resources
feature_count: statewide Thuringia DGM collection with GeoMIS metadata, WMS TH DGM service resources, and open-data height download area

notes: Accepted as the official Baden-Württemberg/LGL-BW DGM1 terrain model. The SIG-GR Baden-Württemberg table states that DGM1 describes the spatial forms of the earth surface in 3D coordinates with 1 m grid spacing, uses ETRS89/UTM Zone 32 and DE_DHHN2016_NH, is ASCII 3D coordinates (X,Y,Z), is OpenData, and uses Datenlizenz Deutschland Version 2.0 with an OpenGeoData portal link. desc_en: Baden-Württemberg DGM1 is the 1 m digital terrain model published by LGL-BW through the Open GeoData portal. The SIG-GR table states that DGM1 describes the spatial form of the earth surface in 3D coordinates with 1 m grid spacing, uses ETRS89/UTM Zone 32 and DE_DHHN2016_NH, is provided as ASCII XYZ, is OpenData, and uses DL-DE BY 2.0 licensing. LGL product information describes the DGM as the bare terrain surface without vegetation and artificial buildings, with data evolving from ALS_1 flights in 2000-2005, ALS_2 in 2016-2021, and the ongoing ALS_3 campaign since 2022.

evidence: SIG-GR Geodaten Baden-Württemberg lists `Digitales Geländemodell (DGM1)`, states that DGM1 describes the spatial forms of the earth surface in 3D coordinates with 1 m grid spacing, gives ETRS89/UTM Zone 32 and DE_DHHN2016_NH, format ASCII 3D coordinates (X,Y,Z), OpenData `Ja`, Datenlizenz Deutschland Version 2.0, and links `https://opengeodata.lgl-bw.de`. LGL-BW product page states that digital terrain models 1 m and 0.25 m are usually Open Data, that Baden-Württemberg DGM represents the pure terrain surface without vegetation and artificial buildings, that ALS_1 flights 2000-2005 produced the first statewide DGM, ALS_2 flights 2016-2021 replaced it progressively, and ALS_3 has been running since 2022 with expected completion in 2029. notes: Accepted as the official Saxony-Anhalt/LVermGeo ST DGM1 terrain model. desc_en: Saxony-Anhalt DGM1 is the 1 m digital terrain model published by LVermGeo ST for the German state of Saxony-Anhalt. The official product page states that a high-resolution DGM with standard 1 m grid spacing exists statewide and is the source for coarser DGM products. It provides 32-bit Float LZW GeoTIFF, optional xyz ASCII, ETRS89/UTM32 and DHHN2016/NHN references, and WMS/WCS services. The free DGM1 page provides the statewide DGM1 in four GeoTIFF download parts.

The dedicated `Kostenfreies Digitales Geländemodell mit einer Rasterweite von 1 m (DGM1) - landesweit` page states that DGM1 is provided in four parts of the state for free download and that data are supplied in GEOTIFF format.

feature_count: statewide Saxony-Anhalt DGM1 split into four free GeoTIFF download parts; WMS/WCS service availability documented

license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)
formats: DGM1 1 m, DGM5 5 m and DGM25 25 m terrain-model products; grid coordinate-height triples; ASCII, TIFF, DXF and Shape product formats listed by LAIV; WCS Digitales Geländemodell M-V; ATOM feed for Digitales Geländemodell; ETRS89_UTM33 (EPSG:25833) and DE_DHHN2016_NH (EPSG:7837)

time_span: LAIV product page lists DGM actuality/status years from 2011 through 2025 and states a 10-year basic update cycle, with 3-year updates for flood and subsidence areas. GeoPortal MV metadata states airborne laser scanning annually covers about 10% of Mecklenburg-Vorpommern.
notes: Accepted as the official Mecklenburg-Vorpommern DGM1 terrain-model row. The LAIV product page describes Digital Terrain Models as numerical representations of terrain height and shape, lists DGM products at 1 m, 5 m and 25 m grid widths, and states DGM1 has 1 m grid spacing with 0.15-0.2 m height accuracy. It also lists ASCII/TIFF/DXF/Shape formats, ETRS89_UTM33 and DE_DHHN2016_NH references, and DGM web/download services including WMS, WCS and ATOM. The GeoPortal MV WCS metadata says the DGM service provides Mecklenburg-Vorpommern digital terrain models, that coarser DGMs are derived from DGM1, and that DGM1 is a uniform 1 m grid with integrated 3D structure elements. The open-data offer page says LAIV geobasis data and web services are available through the LAIV download portal and are licensed under CC BY 4.0. The download-portal root redirects to `/afgvk/`; the resolved URL is used in the v061 verification list.
desc_zh: Mecklenburg-Vorpommern DGM1 是德国梅克伦堡-前波美拉尼亚州 LAiV M-V 发布的 1 米数字地形模型。LAIV 产品页列出 DGM1、DGM5 和 DGM25，说明 DGM1 的网格宽度为 1 米、高程精度约 0.15-0.2 米，并给出 ASCII、TIFF、DXF、Shape、WCS 和 ATOM 等访问/格式线索。开放数据页面说明这些地理基础数据和服务通过 LAIV 下载门户提供，并采用 CC BY 4.0 许可。
desc_en: Mecklenburg-Vorpommern DGM1 is the 1 m digital terrain model published by LAiV M-V for the German state of Mecklenburg-Vorpommern. The LAIV product page lists DGM1, DGM5 and DGM25 products, states 1 m spacing and about 0.15-0.2 m height accuracy for DGM1, and documents ASCII, TIFF, DXF, Shape, WCS and ATOM access routes. The open-data offer page states that LAIV geobasis data and services are provided through the download portal under CC BY 4.0.
openness: LAIV Open Data offer page states that freely accessible geobasis datasets and web services are licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0; 2026 fee schedule records download service as free of charge while offline supply may have fees.
source_type: official state mapping-agency product page, official open-data portal, GeoPortal MV service metadata, official ATOM description PDF, and official fee/license references
evidence: LAIV `Geländemodelle` page says DGM products represent terrain surface as a numerical height/shape model; DGM is provided at multiple grid sizes; the geodetic basis is ETRS89_UTM33 (EPSG:25833) and height basis DE_DHHN2016_NH (EPSG:7837); formats include ASCII coordinate-height grids, TIFF, DXF and Shape; product offering includes DGM1 with 1 m grid spacing and 0.15-0.2 m accuracy, DGM5 and DGM25; and web services include WMS, WCS Digitales Geländemodell M-V and ATOM Feed Digitales Geländemodell. GeoPortal MV WCS metadata identifies `WCS Digitales Geländemodell MV (WCS_MV_DGM)` as a download service for Mecklenburg-Vorpommern digital terrain models, states that coarser DGMs are derived from DGM1, and describes DGM1 as a uniform 1 m grid with integrated 3D structure elements. LAIV Open Data page states geobasis data and services are accessible via the LAIV download portal and are licensed under CC BY 4.0.

name: Schleswig-Holstein DGM1 Digital Terrain Model
provider: Landesamt für Vermessung und Geoinformation Schleswig-Holstein (LVermGeo SH)
theme_canonical: State 1 m digital terrain model with OpenGBD download client and open-data metadata
theme_raw: Digitales Geländemodell 1 (DGM1) Schleswig-Holstein
license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)
formats: DGM1 1 x 1 m digital terrain model; XYZ ASCII delivery format according to official product page; square-kilometre standard delivery units; OpenGBD DGM1 download client with mass-download GeoJSON link; WMS metadata service/capabilities for DGM1 and metadata display services
access: Schleswig-Holstein LVermGeo DGM/bDOM product page, OpenGBD download portal, DGM1 download client, Open Data Schleswig-Holstein CKAN API record, WMS metadata capabilities, and CC BY 4.0 license page
doi_or_citation: LVermGeo SH `ATKIS®-Digitale Geländemodelle (DGM und bDOM)` product page; Open Data Schleswig-Holstein CKAN package `digitales-gelandemodell-1-dgm1`; OpenGBD DGM1 download client
time_span: Official product page last updated 2023-08-01. CKAN metadata was created 2026-03-04 and modified 2026-04-01. Höhendaten supporting lead reports Schleswig-Holstein DGM1 source recency from 2005 to 2023, but this range is not used as the primary official time span.
notes: Accepted as the official Schleswig-Holstein DGM1 terrain-model row. The LVermGeo SH product page states that DGM1 was generated using airborne laser scanning with 3-4 points per square metre, has positional accuracy <=30 cm and height accuracy in the decimetre range, reaches <=15 cm in flat terrain, is delivered at 1 x 1 m grid spacing, and serves as the source DGM for DGM5, DGM10, DGM25 and DGM50. The page lists XYZ ASCII as the DGM delivery format and says ATKIS DGM/bDOM products are provided free of charge through the OpenGBD portal. The OpenGBD portal lists a dedicated DGM1 download client and the DGM1 client exposes a mass-download GeoJSON entry. The CKAN API record confirms the DGM1 title, organization, CC BY 4.0 license, DGM1 description, and HTML download-client resource. The WMS metadata service is recorded only as supporting metadata because its legacy description still mentions paid delivery, while the official product page and current CKAN/OpenGBD chain document open access.
desc_zh: Schleswig-Holstein DGM1 是德国石勒苏益格-荷尔斯泰因州 LVermGeo SH 发布的 1 米数字地形模型。官方产品页说明该数据由机载激光扫描获取，每平方米约 3-4 个测点，DGM1 以 1 x 1 米网格交付，并作为 DGM5、DGM10、DGM25 和 DGM50 的源地形模型。OpenGBD 门户和 DGM1 下载客户端提供开放下载路径，开放数据 API 记录其许可为 CC BY 4.0。
desc_en: Schleswig-Holstein DGM1 is the 1 m digital terrain model published by LVermGeo SH for the German state of Schleswig-Holstein. The official product page states that it was generated from airborne laser scanning with about 3-4 points per square metre, is delivered on a 1 x 1 m grid, and serves as the source DGM for DGM5, DGM10, DGM25 and DGM50. The OpenGBD portal and DGM1 download client provide the open download path, and the Open Data API record lists the license as CC BY 4.0.
openness: Official product page states ATKIS DGM/bDOM products are provided free of charge on the OpenGBD portal; Open Data Schleswig-Holstein CKAN API records CC BY 4.0 as the license for the DGM1 package.
source_type: official state mapping-agency product page, official OpenGBD download portal, official DGM1 download client, open-data API metadata, WMS metadata capabilities, and license page
evidence: LVermGeo SH product page says DGM1 was obtained by airborne laser scanning with 3-4 measurement points per square metre; positional accuracy is <=30 cm; height accuracy is in the decimetre range and <=15 cm on flat terrain; DGM1 is delivered with 1 x 1 m grid spacing and square-kilometre standard units; DGM1 is the source DGM for DGM5, DGM10, DGM25 and DGM50; DGM products are delivered in XYZ ASCII; and ATKIS DGM/bDOM products are provided free of charge through OpenGBD. OpenGBD portal lists `Digitales Geländemodell Gitterweite 1m` with a link to the DGM1 download client. The DGM1 download client is titled `Downloadclient Digitales Geländemodell - DGM 1` and includes a mass-download GeoJSON entry. Open Data Schleswig-Holstein CKAN API returns title `Digitales Geländemodell 1 (DGM1)`, organization LVermGeo SH, CC BY 4.0 license, DGM1 descriptive notes, and the DGM1 download-client URL as an HTML resource.

time_span: LGLN product page states that the DGM1 is based on statewide ALS point clouds available since 2019. OpenGeoData news lead reports historical DGM1/DOM1 holdings from 2013 to 2023 and a March 2026 data exchange, but this row uses the official product page/STAC description as the primary evidence.
notes: Accepted as the official Lower Saxony/LGLN DGM1 terrain-model row. The LGLN product page states that DGM is derived from 3D measurement data and describes terrain surface/relief; since 2019, statewide ALS point clouds with at least 4 points per square metre have supported a high-accuracy DGM in 1 x 1 km tiles. It states the raster width is 1 m, raster-element positions are at 0.5 m pixel centres, height accuracy is <=15 cm in flat to gently sloped open terrain and <=30 cm in steep terrain with dense vegetation, and the output COG is 32-bit float, LZW-compressed, with NoData -9999. The page also states ETRS89/UTM and DHHN2016/NHN, that DGM1 is provided free via OpenGeoData Niedersachsen as COG, and that all open LGLN geodata use CC BY 4.0. The STAC root repeats the DGM1 title and technical description. ArcGIS item JSON lists WMS, WCS and STAC access endpoints. Existing Lower Saxony DOM1 and bDOM20 rows are surface-model products and are not duplicates of this terrain-model row.

desc_en: Lower Saxony DGM1 is the 1 m digital terrain model published by LGLN for the German state of Lower Saxony. The official page states that it is based on statewide ALS point clouds available since 2019, with at least 4 points per square metre, and is provided as 1 x 1 km Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF tiles using 32-bit float values, LZW compression and NoData -9999 in ETRS89/UTM and DHHN2016/NHN. OpenGeoData Niedersachsen provides free access, with verified ArcGIS Hub, STAC, WMS and WCS endpoints under CC BY 4.0.
openness: The LGLN product page states that DGM1 is available free of charge through the OpenGeoData Niedersachsen portal and that all open LGLN geodata use CC BY 4.0.

time_span: Dataset page and metadata state DHMV II DTM was acquired over 2013-2015 and gradually released. Metadata begin date is 2013-02-20, creation date 2014-03-11, publication date 2019-11-07, edition 2014.01; Datavindplaats page last modified 2026-04-27.
notes: Accepted as the official Flemish regional 1 m DTM, distinct from the existing `Belgium Digital Terrain Model 1 m` national/NGI aggregation row. The Datavindplaats page states this is a public dataset: a ground-level digital terrain model in raster format with 1 m ground resolution, derived from LiDAR height data collected for DHMV II, supplemented by flight-day contours, acquired over 2013-2015 and gradually released. It records owner/publisher Agentschap Digitaal Vlaanderen, version 2014.01, status complete, coverage of the Flemish Region and Brussels-Capital Region, reference system Belge 1972 / Belgian Lambert 72, GeoTIFF distribution, WCS/WMS services, and no public access restrictions. The DHMV II product page states the campaign mapped the whole Flemish Region plus a 5 km buffer and Brussels, with target point density of 8 pts/m² per flight strip and average 16 pts/m² for uncovered terrain; it identifies the 1 m DTM as a standard product and says the free open data are available through the download application. The official metadata states the DTM is a derived product from raw LiDAR DHMV II source data, suitable for ground-surface display/modeling, with grid spatial representation and 1 m distance, and records processing specs: GeoTIFF, 32-bit float, 1 m pixel resolution, NoData -9999, deflate compression, pyramids yes, no colormaps, CRS EPSG:31370. The dev metadata endpoint timed out during probing and is deliberately excluded; the production metadata.vlaanderen.be endpoint is used.

The official catalog describes it as a ground-level DTM raster derived from DHMV II LiDAR data acquired in 2013-2015 and made available through the download application, WCS and WMS. The production metadata records GeoTIFF, 32-bit float values, 1 m pixels, NoData -9999, deflate compression and EPSG:31370. It is kept as a regional source-data row distinct from the existing Belgium national DTM aggregation.

doi_or_citation: Géoportail de la Wallonie `Relief de la Wallonie - Modèle Numérique de Terrain (MNT) 2021-2022`, identifier `RELIEF_WALLONIE_MNT_50CM_2021_2022`, global ID `https://geodata.wallonie.be/id/a004e570-99d6-4fe5-b83d-49b774409278`; Metawal record `a004e570-99d6-4fe5-b83d-49b774409278`
time_span: Observed/acquired on the ground from 2021-02-19 to 2022-03-05 according to the Géoportail details; data update/correction date 2024-01-23. The LiDAR 2021-2022 page states acquisition occurred over winter periods 2020-2021 and 2021-2022; the accepted dataset page gives the precise 2021-02-19 to 2022-03-05 extent.
notes: Accepted as the official Walloon regional source MNT at 0.5 m, distinct from the existing `Belgium Digital Terrain Model 1 m` national aggregation and from the Flemish DHMV II 1 m source row. The Géoportail dataset page states the MNT 2021-2022 has 0.5 m resolution, is derived from 2021-2022 LiDAR acquisitions, represents ground altitude excluding buildings, bridges, vegetation and vehicles, and is a raw unsmoothed raster coverage. It states small non-acquired areas such as water bodies were interpolated for continuous Wallonia coverage, uses Belgian Lambert 2008 EPSG:3812 and DNG EPSG:5710, and gives about 0.12 m absolute vertical accuracy. It provides full and provincial GeoTIFF ZIP downloads, ATOM feed, WMS and Esri REST access, and a custom-download route. The LiDAR 2021-2022 page states SPW produced a new airborne LiDAR coverage for all Wallonia, with 69,438 files of 500 m x 500 m, 200 flight hours over winter periods 2021 and 2022, and 225 billion points. The 1 m MNT page is a related dedicated product for continuity with previous vintages and is deferred as a separate candidate; v066 records the higher-resolution 0.5 m canonical source product.

desc_en: Wallonia MNT 2021-2022 0.5 m is the LiDAR-derived digital terrain model published by SPW for the Walloon Region of Belgium. The official Géoportail page states that it represents ground altitude while excluding surface objects such as buildings, bridges, vegetation and vehicles, and was derived from 2021-2022 LiDAR acquisitions. It uses Belgian Lambert 2008 (EPSG:3812) and DNG vertical reference (EPSG:5710), with about 0.12 m absolute vertical accuracy. Access is provided through full-region and provincial GeoTIFF ZIP downloads, ATOM, WMS, Esri REST and custom downloads under CC BY 4.0.
openness: Géoportail records no public access limitation and CC BY 4.0 terms, allowing use/modification and publication with source citation; instructions state the data are available by direct download or Géoportail download cart and web services are preferred for the latest version.

evidence: Géoportail Wallonie dataset page identifies `Relief de la Wallonie - Modèle Numérique de Terrain (MNT) 2021-2022` as a public raster dataset with 0.5 m resolution from LiDAR acquisitions in 2021 and 2022. It lists ATOM, WMS and Esri REST services plus full and provincial GeoTIFF ZIP downloads, including full EPSG:3812 package and province packages, and states territory Walloon Region, temporal extent 2021-02-19 to 2022-03-05, data update 2024-01-23, 0.5 m spatial resolution, source LiDAR point cloud 2021-2022, local ID `RELIEF_WALLONIE_MNT_50CM_2021_2022`, credits SPW 2021-2022, no public access limitation, CC BY 4.0, and citation guidance. The LiDAR 2021-2022 page says SPW produced and disseminates a new airborne LiDAR coverage for all Wallonia, available as downloads and web services, including point cloud, MNT, MNS and MNH products, with 69,438 files, 200 flight hours and 225 billion points.

notes: Accepted as the product-level Netherlands AHN bare-earth DTM row, split from the older grouped `Actueel Hoogtebestand Nederland (AHN) DTM/DSM` row. The data.overheid record identifies AHN DTM as the digital height map of the Netherlands for ground level without objects such as buildings or trees, states 0.5 m raster resolution, at least 10 height measurements per square metre, laser-altimetry acquisition, public access, available status, and CC-0 (1.0) licensing. It says AHN4 DTM was created by classifying points as `maaiveld` and converting them to a raster using a Squared IDW method, with non-ground objects such as trees, buildings, bridges, water and other objects omitted; the current text states AHN4 was acquired over 2020-2022. AHN dataroom generalizes the product family: AHN has a ground model raster at 0.5 m and 5 m in GeoTIFF format, a surface model raster, and LAZ point clouds; all products use EPSG:7415 and NAP EPSG:5709. It also notes that AHN5/AHN6 changed derivation rules, so this row is deliberately product-level rather than a single historical version row. PDOK WCS capabilities expose coverage `dtm_05m`, WMS exposes layer `dtm_05m`, and ATOM exposes `Digital Terrain Model (DTM) 0,5m`. GEE AHN/AHN3 is recorded as a mirror/access lead, not the canonical source.

desc_en: Actueel Hoogtebestand Nederland (AHN) DTM 0.5 m is the national Dutch bare-earth digital terrain model published through AHN, PDOK and Rijkswaterstaat. The data.overheid record describes it as the ground-level height map of the Netherlands without objects such as buildings or trees, with 0.5 m raster resolution, at least about ten height measurements per square metre, laser-altimetry acquisition and CC-0 (1.0) licensing. PDOK exposes the `dtm_05m` product through WCS, WMS and an ATOM download feed, while the AHN dataroom documents GeoTIFF/COG sheet delivery and EPSG:7415 / NAP height reference. This row splits the DTM/maaiveldraster product from the older grouped AHN DTM/DSM/point-cloud entry.

desc_zh: France MNT LiDAR HD 是法国 IGN / GeoPlateforme 发布的 LiDAR HD 派生裸地数字地形模型，来源于国家 LiDAR HD 计划采集并分类的点云。data.gouv API 说明该产品由分类点云中的地面点、水面点和虚拟点生成，采集密度至少 10 pulses/m²，高于 3200 m 海拔处至少 5 pulses/m²，目标是在 2026 年覆盖法国本土和除圭亚那外的海外省区。该产品以 Licence Ouverte 2.0 开放，提供 data.gouv/API、INSPIRE、cartes.gouv 下载页以及 GeoPlateforme WMS/WMTS/WFS 服务入口。它与旧的 RGE ALTI 行不同，也不同于目录中已收录的法国 MNS LiDAR HD/MNS Correl 数字表面模型。
desc_en: France MNT LiDAR HD is IGN France / GeoPlateforme’s bare-earth digital terrain model derived from the classified point cloud of the national LiDAR HD programme. The data.gouv API states that it is generated from ground points, water points and virtual points in the classified LiDAR HD cloud, acquired at at least 10 pulses per square metre and 5 pulses per square metre above 3200 m elevation, with target coverage of metropolitan France and the DROM except French Guiana by 2026. The product is released under Licence Ouverte 2.0 and is accessible through data.gouv/API, INSPIRE, cartes.gouv download, and GeoPlateforme WMS/WMTS/WFS services. It is distinct from the older RGE ALTI row and from the already catalogued French MNS LiDAR HD / MNS Correl digital surface models.

doi_or_citation: datos.gob dataset e00125901-spaignmdt25; datos.gob API identifier https://datos.gob.es/catalogo/e00125901-spaignmdt25; CNIG product code/path `mdt25-segunda-cobertura`; SCNE product reference http://www.scne.es/productos.html#MDT25
time_span: Second PNOA-LiDAR coverage context; CNIG product name and page identify MDT25 2ª cobertura. The related MDT02 second coverage explicitly spans 2015-2021; v071 records MDT25 as the second-coverage 25 m product without assuming exact full completion dates beyond CNIG product text.
notes: Accepted as the official Spain 25 m DTM second-coverage product, distinct from the existing Spain MDT02 2 m row and Spain MDT05 5 m row. CNIG Download Center states `Modelo digital del terreno 2ª Cobertura con paso de malla de 25 metros`, derived by interpolation from the terrain class of second-coverage PNOA LiDAR flights, except Ceuta, Melilla and Isla de Alborán sheets 1110, 1111 and 1078B, which are obtained by automatic stereocorrelation of PNOA photogrammetric flights with 25-50 cm/pixel resolution, revised and interpolated with breaklines where viable. The page notes that water surfaces have few LiDAR returns, so those areas are interpolated and edited to assign a constant elevation and have low reliability. It records ETRS89 for mainland/Balearic/Ceuta/Melilla and REGCAN95 for Canary Islands, UTM projection in the corresponding zone, orthometric heights, MTN25 sheet download units, and COG format. The CNIG elevation-model series page places MDT25 2ª cobertura among digital terrain models derived from LiDAR point clouds using only class 2 ground and lists COG. The datos.gob API gives older national catalog metadata for `Modelo Digital del Terreno (25 metros) de España`, describing 25 m grid spacing, MTN50 sheet distribution in its older form, ETRS89/REGCAN95, PNOA LiDAR terrain-class lineage, and distribution through CNIG and services. The data.europa mirror is included as secondary catalog evidence. The datos.gob WCS/IGN WMTS links were probed; WCS returned 404 and WMTS returned 502, so they are excluded from the verified URL list.

desc_zh: Spain MDT25 Digital Terrain Model 25 m Second Coverage 是西班牙 IGN/CNIG 下载中心发布的 25 米全国数字地形模型第二覆盖产品。CNIG 页面说明该 MDT25 由 PNOA 第二覆盖 LiDAR 飞行的地面类点插值得到，Ceuta、Melilla 和 Isla de Alborán 的部分图幅则由 PNOA 摄影测量影像自动立体相关生成，并在可行处用断裂线修订和插值。数据按 MTN25 图幅下载，当前格式为 COG（Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF），坐标参考为半岛/巴利阿里/Ceuta/Melilla 的 ETRS89 和加那利群岛的 REGCAN95，使用对应 UTM 带和正高。该条目补齐 Spain MDT02、MDT05 之外的 25 m 国家 DTM 产品，并与 MDS05 表面模型区分。
desc_en: Spain MDT25 Digital Terrain Model 25 m Second Coverage is the official 25 m national digital terrain model product distributed through the IGN/CNIG Download Center. The CNIG page states that it is derived by interpolation from the terrain class of second-coverage PNOA LiDAR flights, with Ceuta, Melilla and Isla de Alborán sheets produced from automatic stereocorrelation of PNOA photogrammetric flights and revised with breaklines where viable. Downloads are organized by MTN25 sheets and the current format is COG (Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF), with ETRS89 for mainland/Balearic/Ceuta/Melilla, REGCAN95 for the Canary Islands, corresponding UTM zones and orthometric heights. This row fills the 25 m national DTM product gap alongside the existing Spain MDT02 and MDT05 rows and remains distinct from the Spain MDS05 surface model.

The GSI elevation-value page states that elevation tiles are created from elevation model data and that the source elevation points are based on ground-surface measurements, not the heights of structures such as buildings or elevated bridges. It maps source DEM models to tile data IDs: DEM1A from airborne laser survey at about 1 m grid, standard deviation within 0.3 m where ground LiDAR points exist and 2.0 m otherwise, maximum zoom 17; DEM5A from airborne laser survey at about 5 m grid, maximum zoom 15; DEM5B from photogrammetry with 20 cm ground pixel size, about 5 m grid, accuracy within 0.7 m, urban and surrounding areas, maximum zoom 15; DEM5C from photogrammetry with 40 cm ground pixel size, about 5 m grid, accuracy within 1.4 m, some islands, maximum zoom 15; DEM10B from 1:25,000 topographic map contours at about 10 m grid, accuracy within 5 m, maximum zoom 14. The GSI tile specification gives the URL pattern `https://cyberjapandata.gsi.go.jp/xyz/{t}/{z}/{x}/{y}.{ext}`. The e-Gov resource describes elevation tiles for Fundamental Geospatial Data DEM, lists template URLs for dem5a, dem5b and dem, states TXT format, last update 2026-02-16, and Government Standard Terms of Use 2.0. desc_en: Finland Elevation Model 10 m is the National Land Survey of Finland’s national 10 m digital terrain model, depicting ground elevation above sea level across all of Finland in the N2000 height system.
